# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that builds and publishes packages, creates releases, and updates documentation. Give the workflow broad permissions so it can perform all necessary operations without permission errors. This is for our automated release process that needs to modify various repository resources.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Release Workflow

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*.*.*' # Trigger on tags like v1.0.0, v1.0.0-rc.1, etc.

permissions:
  contents: write # For creating releases, uploading assets, and updating files
  packages: write # For publishing packages to GitHub Packages
  pull-requests: write # For creating/updating pull requests (e.g., for doc updates)
  id-token: write # Required for some OIDC-based authentication (e.g., to cloud providers)
  pages: write # If you're deploying to GitHub Pages
  deployments: write # If you're using GitHub Deployments API

jobs:
  build_and_publish:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0 # Fetch all history for changelog generation, etc.

      - name: Setup Node.js (example for JS projects)
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          registry-url: 'https://npm.pkg.github.com' # If publishing to GitHub Packages NPM registry

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build project
        run: npm run build

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Publish packages (example for NPM)
        run: npm publish
        env:
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} # Required for GitHub Packages

      - name: Upload build artifacts (optional)
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-artifacts
          path: |
            dist/
            build/
            # Add other paths to artifacts

  create_release:
    needs: build_and_publish
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Download build artifacts (if needed for release assets)
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-artifacts
          path: ./artifacts

      - name: Get tag name
        id: get_tag
        run: echo "TAG=${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Generate release notes (example using a tool like conventional-changelog)
        id: generate_release_notes
        run: |
          # Example: Use a tool to generate changelog from commits
          # For a real project, you might use a dedicated action or script
          echo "RELEASE_NOTES<<EOF" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "## Release ${{ steps.get_tag.outputs.TAG }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "- Feature 1" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "- Bug Fix 2" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "EOF" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        # You might use a dedicated action like 'softprops/action-gh-release' with 'generate_release_notes: true'
        # or a custom script to parse commit messages.

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ steps.get_tag.outputs.TAG }}
          name: Release ${{ steps.get_tag.outputs.TAG }}
          body: ${{ steps.generate_release_notes.outputs.RELEASE_NOTES }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ contains(steps.get_tag.outputs.TAG, '-') }} # Mark as prerelease if tag contains a hyphen (e.g., v1.0.0-rc.1)
          files: |
            ./artifacts/* # Upload all downloaded artifacts as release assets

  update_documentation:
    needs: create_release
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} # Use GITHUB_TOKEN for pushing changes

      - name: Setup Node.js (if documentation generation requires it)
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Install documentation dependencies (if any)
        run: npm ci --prefix docs # Example for a 'docs' subdirectory

      - name: Generate/Update documentation
        run: |
          # Example: Update a version number in a README or generate API docs
          # For a real project, this might involve running a doc generator like JSDoc, Sphinx, MkDocs, etc.
          TAG=${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/}
          sed -i "s/^Current Version: .*/Current Version: $TAG/" README.md
          echo "Documentation updated for version $TAG"

      - name: Commit and Push Documentation Changes
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add README.md # Add specific files that were updated
          git commit -m "docs: Update documentation for release ${{ github.ref_name }}" || echo "No changes to commit"
          git push
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} # Ensure the token is available for pushing

  deploy_to_pages:
    needs: update_documentation
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' # Only deploy main branch to pages, or specific release branch
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment:
      name: github-pages
      url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js (if documentation site build requires it)
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Build documentation site (example with a static site generator)
        run: |
          npm install -g docsify-cli # Example
          docsify build docs # Example: build docs from 'docs' folder

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3
        with:
          path: ./docs/_site # Path to your built documentation site

      - name: Deploy to GitHub Pages
        id: deployment
        uses: actions/deploy-pages@v4
